返回本批次

现在是手工编写代码的最佳时机

Now is the best time to write code by hand

sitebloom.ch 作者 nickgreg 分数 69 评论 32 2026/04/11 19:24
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 人工智能意味着更多的人可以编写代码。人工智能还意味着能够编写代码的人将会减少。大量使用法学硕士有三个主要动机。首先，有关超高速运输的推文热潮带来的社会压力。尽管它们很夸张，但不可否认的是，创建工作原型的速度有多快。如果您选择忽略工具，您将在生产力方面被代理工程师抛在后面。一旦你尝试了这些模型，你就会意识到它们有多好，还有第二个激励因素。这些东西编写工作代码，随着模型变得越来越好，关于它们犯错误的争论将变得越来越安静，直到消失，就像所有随着时间的推移而被证明是错误的高度确信的观点一样。第三个动机是最强烈的：人类的懒惰。 AI可以更快地编写代码，让你在繁忙的工作中得到放松。模特提供者就像一个健身房，一旦你走进去，你就会变成超人。为什么要去隔壁的健身房，在那里你举起的重量微乎其微，而且很痛？这三个原因意味着生成的代码更多，手工编写的更少。这意味着工程技能衰退的时间已经开始了。这对自律者来说是一个机会。在获得法学硕士学位之前，愿意刻意练习工程技能的人很少。我们大多数人只会在积极面试时进行练习，但在工作中编写代码意味着随着时间的推移我们会变得更加熟练。现在我们只要求完成工作，保持技能已经成为一项非常费力的活动。由此可见，熟练工程师的数量将会比现在有所下降。这项技能确实很重要。未来10年，最好的公司仍然会聘请能够解决算法难题、在压力下手工构建系统并讨论架构权衡的工程师。我相信最好的产品和突破仍然来自于深刻理解基础知识的人。超级智能会打破这一点，但那是另一篇文章了。假设你不同意我的实践想法，并且你作为个人全力投入代理工程，结果会如何？情况一：你受雇了，你只通过代理编写代码，你的技能萎缩，你的技能水平下降到能够做你所做的事情的人大大增加的水平。作为一名忘记了基础知识的软件工程师，你现在要与产品设计师、业务人员以及“重返舞台”的老经理竞争。你不再是一个特殊的键盘精灵，你只是一只提示猴。现在的情况是，您是一名代理工程师，正在构建副产品或您的初创公司。如果你发现了一种能够成长并具有商业模式的产品，你就可以筹集资金，进入管理层并积累财富。很好的结果，没有抱怨。然而，对于那些没有成长的产品，你已经创造了一个失败的产品。该产品没有提供您可以像两年前那样构建的信号。它只表明你促使了一个想法的存在并试图将其推向市场。狂热的风投们当然喜欢这种模式，因为他们挑选了获胜者，但这对个人来说并不理想。如果每个人都不同意并认为软件工程基础知识的刻意练习就结束了。这就像在福特第一家工厂旁边当蹄铁匠一样好。对我来说一切都更好。

Original Summary

  1. AI means more people can create code. AI also means fewer people will be able to write code. There are three main incentives to use LLMs heavily. First, the social pressure from the fever tweets about hyperspeed shipping. As over the top as they are, it’s undeniable how quickly working prototypes can be created. If you choose to ignore the tools you will be left behind by the agentic engineers in productivity terms. Once you try the models, you realise how good they are, and there is the second incentive. These things write working code and as the models get better and better, the argument that they make mistakes will get quieter and quieter until it fades away, like all high conviction opinions that turn out to be wrong over time do. The third incentive is the strongest of all: human laziness. AI can write the code faster and you can relax with busywork. The model providers are like a gym that makes you Superman once you step inside. Why go to the gym next door where it hurts and you lift measly little weights? These three reasons mean more code is produced and less is written by hand. And that means the clock has already started on engineering skills atrophying. And that is an opportunity for the disciplined. The pool of people who would deliberately practice their engineering skills was small before LLMs. The majority of us would only practice when actively interviewing, but writing code in our jobs meant we became more skilled over time. Now that we only ask for the work to be done, remaining skilled has become a very high effort activity. It follows that the number of skilled engineers will drop from what it is today. And that skill does matter. For the next 10 years, the best companies will still hire engineers who can solve algorithmic puzzles, build systems by hand under pressure, and discuss architecture trade-offs. I believe the best products and breakthroughs will still come from people who deeply understand fundamentals. Super intelligence would break that, but that’s a different essay. Let’s say you disagree with my idea to practice, and you go all in on agentic engineering as an individual, how might that turn out? Case one: you are employed, you only write code through agents, your skills atrophy and you move down to a skill level where the pool of people capable of doing what you do has increased enormously. As a software engineer who has forgotten the fundamentals, you now compete with product designers, business people, and your old manager who is “back in the arena”. You are no longer a special wizard of the keyboard, you are just a prompt monkey. Now the case where you’re an agentic engineer building side products or your startup. If you hit on a product that grows and has a business model, you can raise money, move into management, and build wealth. Great result, no complaints. However, for those that don’t grow, you have created a failed product. The product does not provide the signal that you can build like it did two years ago. It shows very little other than that you prompted an idea into existence and tried to market it. The fever-tweeting VCs of course love this model as they pick up the winners, but its not ideal for the individual. If everyone disagrees and thinks deliberate practice of software engineering fundamentals is over. That it’s like being a farrier next to Ford’s first factory then good. All the better for me.

评论（默认中文）

gitaarik 2026/04/12 15:42
我不知道，当我开始担任 Web 开发人员时，我曾经遇到过一位铁杆 C 开发人员，他的低级知识给我留下了深刻的印象，并且最终也想学习这些知识。但随着时间的推移，网络世界不断发展并变得越来越有趣，最终我想制作应用程序。几年后，我再次见到这个人，他告诉我他进入了 Ruby on Rails 业务，因为那里有很多工作。
I don't know, when I started out as a web developer I met a hardcore C developer once and I was quite impressed about his low level knowledge, and wanted to eventually learn that too. But as time passed by the web world kept developping and becoming more interesting and eventually I want to make applications in the end. A few years later I saw the guy again and he told me he got into Ruby on Rails businesses, because there was a lot of work in there.
在 HN 中查看
elephanlemon 2026/04/12 09:29
目前，专门练习代码是工程师的众多选择之一。其他技能怎么样？例如，现在似乎是一个开始在特定领域开发深入知识的好机会，这样当你在该领域构建人工智能辅助软件时，你就有足够的能力知道它是否在做正确的事情。或者，更好地理解一系列学科，这样当你去解决问题时，你就能意识到它们并有更多的领域可以借鉴。 （我相信这种组合就是 Valve 所说的 T 型员工。）也是发展人际交往能力的好机会。
Practicing code specifically is one of many options for engineers right now. How about other skills? For example, now seems like a good opportunity to start developing deep knowledge in a particular domain, so that when you build AI assisted software in that space, you’re competent enough to know if it’s doing the right thing. Or, develop a better understanding of a range of disciplines, so that when you go to solve problems, you’re aware of them and have more areas to draw from. (The combination is what Valve calls a T-shaped employee I believe.) Also a good opportunity to develop your interpersonal skills.
在 HN 中查看
ehnto 2026/04/12 14:31
我同意。我认为快速学习通才现在具有真正的优势，但这种优势不能被那些专门聘用专家的大公司所利用。我认为这就是为什么大团队中的个人贡献者没有从人工智能中看到巨大的好处，而小团队或单独的开发人员可能会看到更大的影响力。

如果你是一位具有创业精神的多面手，我想我的目标是被一家小公司聘用，在那里你可以提供大量的价值，或者考虑开始一些你拥有软件之外领域经验的事情。

I agree. I think the rapid learning generalist has a real advantage right now, but that kind of advantage cannot be leveraged by big companies structured to utilise specialists. I think that's why individual contributors in big teams aren't seeing massive benefits from AI where a small team or solo developer may be seeing greater leverage.

If you are a strong generalist with an entrepreneurial spirit, I think I would be aiming at getting hired by a small company where you can provide a buttload of value or looking at starting something where you have domain experience outside of software.

在 HN 中查看
Nevermark 2026/04/12 14:49
> 我认为这就是为什么大团队中的个人贡献者没有从人工智能中看到巨大的好处，而小团队或单独的开发人员可能会看到更大的影响力。

这听起来是真的。对于小团队来说，这是有史以来最好的时光。一个大团队可能由几个较小的团队组成，因此这对他们来说也可以是力量倍增器。

重组大型团队的另一个力量倍增器是愿意考虑从单一贡献者开始的小型团队。

这是最糟糕的时期：适应缓慢。

> I think that's why individual contributors in big teams aren't seeing massive benefits from AI where a small team or solo developer may be seeing greater leverage.

This rings true. It is the best time ever for small teams. A big team is potentially several smaller teams, so this can be a force multiplier for them too.

Another force multiplier for reorganizing larger teams, be willing to consider smaller teams starting with single contributors.

What this is the worst time for: slow adaptation.

在 HN 中查看
sph 2026/04/12 14:46
从定义上来说，通才更有能力，但大公司不需要广泛的能力，他们只需要一个齿轮。
Generalists are more competent by definition, but large companies don’t need broad competence, they just need a cog.
在 HN 中查看
bvan 2026/04/12 05:22
完全同意。特别是对于增加价值的商业或代码。编写代码只是开发优质、健壮的软件的要素之一。在现实世界中，商业或生产软件必须得到维护、支持，并且必须响应不断变化的用户需求。人的因素是至关重要的，除非你愿意依赖法学硕士，祈祷，并且不关心支持用户。
Totally agree. Especially for commercial or code that adds value. Writing code is just one element of developing quality, robust, software. In the rea world, commercial or production software must be maintained, supported, and must respond to changing user requirements. The human element is critical, unless you’re OK with relying on LLM’s, crossing your fingers, and have no care to support users.

hoppp 2026/04/12 07:51

编码赋予创造力优势

What I've been noticing is the abundance of the same "revolutionary" idea spoon fed by claude to everyone and their mom.

Coding gives the edge in creativity

cerealizer 2026/04/12 03:36
是的，我只想要手工制作、手工、小批量、散养、有机代码。
Yes, I only want hand crafted, artisanal, small-batch, free-range, organic code.
在 HN 中查看
sph 2026/04/12 14:40
但这是讽刺性的。把你的批量生产的泥水留给你自己，让我看看你通过努力建造了什么。

我花了两天时间学习四元数来修复一艘在太空中移动的有缺陷的宇宙飞船，而法学硕士可以在几秒钟内为我完成这件事。很好地利用了我的时间：现在我了解了四元数，并且我的知识水平得到了提高，这使我更有信心解决这个领域的更大问题。

This but unironically. Keep your mass-produced slop for yourself, show me what you have built with effort.

I spent two days learning about quaternions to fix a buggy spaceship moving in space, when an LLM could have done it for me in seconds. A great use of my time: now I understand quaternions and I have levelled up in my knowledge, which makes me more confident to tackle larger problems in this space.

jditu 2026/04/12 12:20 deleted

在 HN 中查看
tedd4u 2026/04/12 06:10
我认为您必须从 55 岁以上开始，然后向上找到一个年龄范围，其中超过 10% 的程序员在其职业生涯中经常编写汇编代码。

要找到相同的机器代码，您需要从 65 岁或以上开始。

I think you'd have to start with 55+ years old and go upward to find an age range where more than 10% of programmers routinely wrote assembler code in their careers.

To find the same for machine code you'd need to start at 65 or older.

在 HN 中查看
somewhereoutth 2026/04/12 08:46
确实不太一样。汇编/机器代码完全是确定性的——它们是你想法的符号。 LLM制作的内容更多的是其他人思想的大杂烩，无法帮助你清晰、确信等。
Really not the same. Assembly / machine code is entirely deterministic - they are a notation for your thoughts. LLM produced content is more a smorgasbord of other people's thoughts, and cannot help you with clarity, conviction, etc etc.
在 HN 中查看
lmz 2026/04/12 09:23
是的，汇编是确定性的（除非出现严重的硬件错误）。但这就是重点。人们不再编写 Assembly。
Yes Assembly is deterministic (barring severe hardware bugs). But that's the point. People are no longer writing Assembly.
在 HN 中查看
xigoi 2026/04/12 15:45
他们的意思是说，从汇编转向高级编程与从高级编程转向法学硕士不同，因为后者会让你无法保证计算机会按照你的指示去做。
They meant to say that swithing from assembly to high-level programming is not the same as switching from high-level programming to LLMs, because the latter loses you the guarantee that the computer will do what you told it to.
在 HN 中查看
budman1 2026/04/12 06:15
如果将 10% 改为 0.5%，我会同意。 我今年 62 岁，从事低级编码和硬件接口工作。 “通常”甚至不；我想说有时需要看一下，或者更罕见的是，必须写一点（比如一个小模块）
change that 10% to 0.5% and I would agree. i am 62, worked in low level coding and hw interfacing. 'routinely' not even; i would say on occasion, needed to look at a bit, or even more rare, had to write a bit (like a small module)
在 HN 中查看
spoiler 2026/04/12 06:50
好奇，你平时用什么？我必须编写一些对时间敏感的 MC 驱动程序，而我知道如何可靠地做到这一点的唯一方法是使用汇编。但当然，这并不_经常_，只是超出了我的预期（特别是对于那些通常不做低级工作的人来说，这是一个艺术项目）
Curious, what do you normally use? I had to write a few timing sensitive MC drivers and the only way I knew how onto do that reliably was using assembly. But granted, it wasn't _often_, just more than I expected (especially for someone who doesn't normally do that low level stuff, this was for an art project)
在 HN 中查看
budman1 2026/04/12 07:09
当然。 时间敏感的东西。 < 50 行。 一旦关键的事情结束就跳回C。

“性能的东西”。 由于多种原因，我尝试用 C 语言解决它；其他可读性是其中之一。 几乎不需要做更多的事情，而只是嵌入 C 中的一个简短的汇编宏。

在汇编中，have 的实际用途最多的是“这里发生了什么……”，并且需要向汇编的调试器询问一些更深入的理解。

有些年头，这些事情做了 5 次，所以可能需要 20 个小时。 其他年份，从来没有。

至于“坐下来编写一些程序集来解决问题 X”，答案是否定的。 （X 位于上述各项的中间时除外）

sure. timing sensitive stuff. < 50 lines. jump back to C as soon as the critical stuff is over.

'performance stuff'. i try to solve it in C for a bunch of reasons; others readability is one. almost never need to do more than a short macro of assembly embedded in C.

the actual most use of have for assembly is "what is happening here.." and need to ask the debugger for the assembly for some deeper understanding.

Some years, did these things 5 times, so maybe 20 hours. Other years, never.

As far as "sit down and write some assembly to solve problem X", the answer is never. (except when X is right in the middle of the above items)

在 HN 中查看
shric 2026/04/12 07:28
是的，我父亲现在 70 岁了，我确实记得他在 80 年代末写过汇编语言，但此后就没有了。
Yeah, my father is now 70, I do remember he wrote assembly language in the late 80s but not since.
在 HN 中查看
sitzkrieg 2026/04/12 09:46
39 岁，拥有 10 多年编写程序集的经验。嗯？
39 and have over 10 years writing assembly. huh?
在 HN 中查看
ares623 2026/04/12 15:07
我知道最近人们经常提到脑萎缩。但有人担心早发性痴呆吗？如果我们在未来几十年内看到更多的年轻痴呆症患者需要护理，那么下一代将再次承担这个负担，这样当代一代就可以做到“lulz”。耶稣跳踢踏舞天哪，这项工作一开始就没有那么难。
I know brain atrophy is mentioned a lot these days. But is anyone worried about early onset dementia? If we see a higher number of younger dementia patients needing care in the next few decades, once again it will be the next generation who will bear the burden just so the current generation can do the "lulz". Jesus tap-dancing Christ the job wasn't even that hard in the first place.
在 HN 中查看
somewhereoutth 2026/04/12 08:38
还有散文和素描。

所有这些东西（代码、散文、草图）都是关于 通过制作思考。

and prose, and sketching.

All these things (code, prose, sketching) are about thinking through making.

在 HN 中查看
sigseg1v 2026/04/12 03:08
这篇文章的目的是说“如果你只做一件事，你可能不会在其他事情上表现出色”吗？有人认为这不是一个显而易见的结论吗？我错过重点了吗？
Is the purpose of this article to say "If you only do one thing, you will likely not excel at other things"? Is there anyone to which this is not an obvious conclusion? Did I miss the point?
在 HN 中查看
layer8 2026/04/12 07:04
我想你做到了。争论（可能是错误的）是，代理编码的进入门槛比手动编码更低，并且由于（除非 AGI）仍然存在对手动编码的需求，因此开发人员失去的技能越多，该技能就会变得更有价值，而代理编码由于其较低的进入门槛将变得不那么有价值。
I think you did. The argument (which may be wrong) is that agentic coding has a lower barrier to entry than hand-coding, and that since (barring AGI) there will remain a demand for hand-coding, that skill will become more valuable the more developers lose it, while agentic coding due to its lower barrier to entry will become less valuable.
在 HN 中查看
ofjcihen 2026/04/12 05:22
你做到了。

关键是，如果你让某些事情替你思考 x，你就会变得更不擅长思考 x。

You did.

The point is if you let something think about x for you you will become worse at thinking about x.

在 HN 中查看
SpicyLemonZest 2026/04/12 09:13
这篇文章的目的是说软件工程的技能取决于 能力 手动编写代码，即使现在您不一定需要每天这样做。作者认为，如果你不坚持实践，即使在驾驶代理方面，你也会比那些坚持实践的人更有效。

这是真的吗？我不像作者那么有信心，但似乎是有道理的。我见过许多曾经编写代码的经理试图推动克劳德，甚至向他们报告的初级工程师也失败了。

The purpose of the article is to say that the skill of software engineering depends on the ability to write code by hand, even now that you don't necessarily have to on a day to day basis. If you don't keep in practice, the author thinks, you will become less effective even at driving agents than people who do.

Is that true? I'm not quite as confident as the author, but it seems plausible. I've seen a number of managers who used to write code try and fail to drive Claude as well as even the junior engineers reporting to them.

在 HN 中查看
oliver236 2026/04/12 02:48
真是太糟糕了。
such a bad take.
在 HN 中查看
